channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal agencies begin removing DEI guidance from websites in Trump crackdown
item.description: Several federal agencies have begun removing resources for underrepresented Americans from their webpages following President Donald Trump’s executive order cracking down on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Alia Wong, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders in debt limit quandary between Trump, fiscal hawks
item.description: Congressional GOP leaders are in a massive pickle as they weigh how to deal with a looming deadline to raise the debt ceiling. President Trump is pressuring Republicans to not allow Democrats to use the borrowing limit as a leverage point.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Senate panel sets hearing on Tulsi Gabbard nomination
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee has scheduled a hearing to review the nomination of Tulsi Gabbard to serve as director of national intelligence. The Jan.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Experts warn about the ’crumbling infrastructure’ of federal government data
item.description: Unstable federal funding puts at risk the government statistics used to track the U.S. economy and population, officials and data users warn. That’s before any cuts by President Trump and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance speaks at March for Life rally in Washington
item.description: A rally is scheduled for noon on the National Mall and we expect a string of Republicans to speak about their anti-abortion plans.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Florida's special congressional primaries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Control of the U.S. House isn’t on the line in Tuesday’s special congressional primaries in Florida, but Republicans are still eager to find replacements for former GOP Reps. Matt Gaetz and Michael Waltz to add some breathing room to their slender majority in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk tried to turn the salute controversy into a joke
item.description: The incident only lasted seconds, but it sparked what has become a global debate about how to interpret what Musk did. Then Musk started posting Nazi-themed jokes.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Tenn. Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve 3rd term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces pivotal moment with California trip
item.description: President Trump will venture into deep-blue California on Friday for a closely-watched visit that could determine disaster aid for the state and how Trump will work with Democratic governors throughout his four years in office.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats' Minnesota House boycott echoes earlier walkouts in other states
item.description: Democrats in the Minnesota House who have boycotted daily sessions are using tactics that lawmakers elsewhere have tried dozens of times in seeking to thwart their opponents.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna And Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump and Vance join March for Life anti-abortion activists in celebrating the movement's gains
item.description: President Donald Trump and his vice president, JD Vance, are celebrating gains in the anti-abortion movement with activists in the annual March for Life.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Christine Fernando And Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
